name: file_patch
description: Apply Python-based multi-line string replacements and other file modifications in one step. Supports replacing old text with new text (including multi-line), inserting before/after a pattern, deleting matching lines, appending, and prepending. Uses Python for reliable multi-line handling unlike sed. Replaces the common pattern of writing a Python script to /tmp and executing it to patch files.
when_to_use: Use when you need to modify source code files with complex multi-line replacements that sed cannot handle reliably. Replaces the pattern of writing a Python script to /tmp (e.g., cat > /tmp/patch_*.py << 'PYEOF' ... PYEOF && python3 /tmp/patch_*.py) with a single script call. Particularly useful for TypeScript, Go, Python, and Rust source files where you need to replace multi-line code blocks, add fields to classes/interfaces, or update function bodies. Supports: replace (old -> new), insert-before, insert-after, delete-matching, append, prepend.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the file to modify

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'replace' (replace old text with new text), 'insert-before' (insert text before matching pattern), 'insert-after' (insert text after matching pattern), 'delete-matching' (delete lines containing pattern), 'append' (append text to end of file), 'prepend' (prepend text to beginning of file)

parameters.name: old_or_pattern
parameters.description: For 'replace': the exact old text to replace (can be multi-line, use \n for newlines). For 'insert-before'/'insert-after'/'delete-matching': the search pattern (substring match). For 'append'/'prepend': the text to add.

parameters.name: new_text
parameters.description: For 'replace': the new text to insert (can be multi-line, use \n for newlines). For 'insert-before'/'insert-after': the text to insert. Not used for 'delete-matching', 'append', 'prepend'.

examples.call: main.sh src/app.ts append '// end of file'
examples.expected: Appends '// end of file' to the end of the file.
Prepends 'package main\n\n' to the beginning of the file.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 4-6 steps (cat writing Python script to /tmp, executing it, and cleanup) with a single script call. Eliminates the error-prone escaping issues of heredoc-based Python scripts. Handles multi-line replacements reliably using Python's string processing, avoiding sed's fragility with special characters and multi-line patterns. Cuts output token volume by not showing the Python script content.
